Why are you interested in this role?: Onboarding isn't just about getting someone set up—from my experience, it's about understanding what a customer is really trying to do, guiding them to their first win, and refining the path with empathy and data so they keep moving. That's a rhythm I already live in. At InGenius Prep I guide customers and stakeholders through onboarding milestones across 20+ programs and built documentation that cut onboarding time by 75%. I genuinely like the moment someone goes from confused to confident—it's the most rewarding part of the work for me. A Jr. Onboarding Associate role is that, focused on helping a real business owner reach something concrete like their first payroll, and that's exactly where my energy goes.
Why do you want to work at Gusto?: The mission lands for me—Gusto helping small businesses run payroll and take care of their people, and the fact that this role is about getting an owner to a real, concrete milestone rather than just closing a ticket. That matches how I'm wired: I'm a people-loving, consultative problem solver who likes meeting someone where they are. At Kronos Research I engaged people through personalized communication and increased applications 566% in two months—early proof that I can move someone toward action by genuinely connecting, not pushing. I'd bring that same warmth and patience to small business owners who are often setting up payroll for the first time and need a guide who'll stay with them.
What excites you about this opportunity?: The directness of the impact. Helping a small business owner hit their first payroll is a moment they remember—and being the person who makes that feel easy instead of intimidating is genuinely motivating. I also like that it's a data-informed, AI-enabled, customer-first role: at InGenius I use AI tools daily for communications and feedback analysis, and I'm used to letting signal—not assumptions—shape how I help someone. At NextGen I analyzed 2,000+ experiences specifically to amplify customer voice. So the part that excites me is doing the human, consultative work and refining the onboarding path with real data so each customer's first win comes faster than the last.
Tell us about a time you helped someone: At Kronos Research, the challenge was a thin applicant pipeline. Instead of blasting generic outreach, I engaged talent through personalized communication and customer-service-style follow-up—meeting people where they actually were in their decision. Applications increased 566% in two months. The lesson stuck with me: people move toward action when they feel genuinely understood and guided, not pressured. That's the same instinct I'd bring to onboarding a Gusto customer—read what they're really trying to accomplish, walk them to that first concrete win, and make the path feel supported the whole way. The metric matters, but the trust I build getting there is what makes it repeatable.
